req.title: Message to provider
req.statement: A patient shall send a message to their provider; messages containing attachments shall scan for disallowed PHI exfiltration patterns.

doc.reasoning: R-HLT-025 covers three distinct risk zones: (1) a valid message send with no attachment — happy path confirming storage and provider notification; (2) a message with an attachment containing disallowed PHI exfiltration patterns — must be blocked; (3) a boundary case at the exact maximum allowed attachment size with a clean file — system must accept without false-positive PHI rejection. EP drives the positive case (valid partition), NEG drives the blocked-attachment case, BVA drives the max-size boundary. An additional STC test covers the full state machine: draft → sent → provider-notified. A DT test covers the decision matrix of attachment present × PHI detected combinations.

tests.name: test_send_plain_text_message_stores_and_notifies
preconditions: Patient is authenticated and session cookie is valid
Provider exists in the system with a known provider_id
No prior unread messages exist for the provider (idempotent via <fixture_clean_inbox>)
actions: POST /messages with body {subject, body_text, provider_id} and no attachment
Assert HTTP 201 and capture message_id from response
GET /messages/{message_id} to verify storage
GET /notifications?provider_id=<provider_id> to verify notification created
expected: HTTP 201 returned with a non-null message_id
GET /messages/{message_id} returns 200 with matching subject and body
Provider notification record exists with status='unread'
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_send_plain_text_message_stores_and_notifies(
    authed_patient_session,   # <fixture>
    api_client,               # <fixture>
    clean_inbox,              # <fixture>
    provider_id,              # <fixture>
):
    payload = {
        "subject": "Follow-up question",
        "body": "When should I take the medication?",
        "provider_id": provider_id,
    }
    resp = api_client.post("/messages", json=payload)
    assert resp.status_code == 201
    msg_id = resp.json()["message_id"]
    assert msg_id

    get_resp = api_client.get(f"/messages/{msg_id}")
    assert get_resp.status_code == 200
    data = get_resp.json()
    assert data["subject"] == payload["subject"]
    assert data["body"] == payload["body"]

    notif_resp = api_client.get("/notifications", params={"provider_id": provider_id})
    assert notif_resp.status_code == 200
    notifs = notif_resp.json()["notifications"]
    assert any(n["message_id"] == msg_id and n["status"] == "unread" for n in notifs)

tests.name: test_attachment_with_phi_pattern_is_blocked
preconditions: Patient is authenticated and session cookie is valid
Provider exists in the system with a known provider_id
A PDF fixture containing SSN and DOB PHI exfiltration patterns is available via <phi_attachment_fixture>
actions: POST /messages with a multipart body containing subject, body_text, provider_id, and the PHI-laden attachment
Assert HTTP 422 or 400 is returned
Assert response body contains a rejection reason referencing PHI policy
GET /messages to confirm no new message was stored
expected: Response JSON contains error code 'PHI_EXFILTRATION_DETECTED' or equivalent
No message record created in the system
trace: R-HLT-025: blocked when disallowed attachment detected
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_attachment_with_phi_pattern_is_blocked(
    authed_patient_session,   # <fixture>
    api_client,               # <fixture>
    phi_attachment_fixture,   # <fixture> returns (filename, bytes) with SSN/DOB patterns
    provider_id,              # <fixture>
):
    fname, fbytes = phi_attachment_fixture
    files = {"attachment": (fname, fbytes, "application/pdf")}
    data = {
        "subject": "Test PHI attachment",
        "body": "See attached.",
        "provider_id": provider_id,
    }
    resp = api_client.post("/messages", data=data, files=files)
    assert resp.status_code in (400, 422)
    body = resp.json()
    assert "PHI_EXFILTRATION_DETECTED" in body.get("error_code", "")

    list_resp = api_client.get("/messages")
    messages = list_resp.json().get("messages", [])
    subjects = [m["subject"] for m in messages]
    assert "Test PHI attachment" not in subjects

tests.name: test_attachment_at_max_allowed_size_without_phi_is_accepted
preconditions: Patient is authenticated and session cookie is valid
Provider exists in the system with a known provider_id
System max attachment size is known (e.g., 10 MB); fixture generates a clean file of exactly that size via <max_size_clean_attachment_fixture>
actions: POST /messages with a multipart body containing a clean attachment of exactly MAX_ATTACHMENT_BYTES
Assert HTTP 201 is returned
GET /messages/{message_id} and verify attachment metadata reflects the correct size
Confirm no PHI rejection error is raised
expected: HTTP 201 returned — boundary-size clean attachment accepted
trace: R-HLT-025: messages containing attachments shall scan for disallowed PHI exfiltration patterns
tests.executable:
import pytest

MAX_ATTACHMENT_BYTES = 10 * 1024 * 1024  # 10 MB per system spec

def test_attachment_at_max_allowed_size_without_phi_is_accepted(
    authed_patient_session,           # <fixture>
    api_client,                       # <fixture>
    max_size_clean_attachment_fixture, # <fixture> returns (filename, bytes) of exactly MAX bytes
    provider_id,                      # <fixture>
    clean_inbox,                      # <fixture>
):
    fname, fbytes = max_size_clean_attachment_fixture
    assert len(fbytes) == MAX_ATTACHMENT_BYTES, "Fixture size mismatch"

    files = {"attachment": (fname, fbytes, "application/octet-stream")}
    data = {
        "subject": "Max size attachment test",
        "body": "Boundary value test — no PHI.",
        "provider_id": provider_id,
    }
    resp = api_client.post("/messages", data=data, files=files)
    assert resp.status_code == 201
    msg_id = resp.json()["message_id"]
    assert msg_id

    get_resp = api_client.get(f"/messages/{msg_id}")
    assert get_resp.status_code == 200
    attachment_meta = get_resp.json().get("attachment", {})
    assert attachment_meta.get("size_bytes") == MAX_ATTACHMENT_BYTES

tests.name: test_message_state_transitions_draft_to_sent_to_notified
preconditions: Patient is authenticated and session cookie is valid
Provider exists in the system with a known provider_id
System supports draft creation via POST /messages/drafts
actions: POST /messages/drafts to create a message in DRAFT state
Assert status='draft' in response
PATCH /messages/drafts/{draft_id}/send to transition to SENT
Assert HTTP 200 and status='sent'
GET /notifications?provider_id=<provider_id> and assert notification with status='unread' exists
expected: Provider notification created with status='unread' after send
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_message_state_transitions_draft_to_sent_to_notified(
    authed_patient_session,  # <fixture>
    api_client,              # <fixture>
    clean_inbox,             # <fixture>
    provider_id,             # <fixture>
):
    draft_payload = {
        "subject": "STC draft test",
        "body": "Draft body.",
        "provider_id": provider_id,
    }
    draft_resp = api_client.post("/messages/drafts", json=draft_payload)
    assert draft_resp.status_code == 201
    draft_data = draft_resp.json()
    assert draft_data["status"] == "draft"
    draft_id = draft_data["draft_id"]

    send_resp = api_client.patch(f"/messages/drafts/{draft_id}/send")
    assert send_resp.status_code == 200
    assert send_resp.json()["status"] == "sent"
    msg_id = send_resp.json()["message_id"]

    notif_resp = api_client.get("/notifications", params={"provider_id": provider_id})
    assert notif_resp.status_code == 200
    notifs = notif_resp.json()["notifications"]
    assert any(n["message_id"] == msg_id and n["status"] == "unread" for n in notifs)

tests.name: test_decision_table_attachment_present_vs_phi_detected
preconditions: Patient is authenticated and session cookie is valid
Provider exists in the system with a known provider_id
Fixtures available: clean_attachment_fixture (no PHI), phi_attachment_fixture (contains PHI patterns)
actions: Case A — No attachment, no PHI: POST plain message → expect 201
Case B — Attachment present, no PHI: POST with clean file → expect 201
Case C — Attachment present, PHI detected: POST with PHI file → expect 400/422
Assert stored message count increments by 2 (Cases A and B only)
expected: Case C: 400 or 422, message NOT stored, PHI_EXFILTRATION_DETECTED in error
trace: R-HLT-025: messages containing attachments shall scan for disallowed PHI exfiltration patterns
R-HLT-025: blocked when disallowed attachment detected
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_decision_table_attachment_present_vs_phi_detected(
    authed_patient_session,    # <fixture>
    api_client,                # <fixture>
    clean_inbox,               # <fixture>
    provider_id,               # <fixture>
    clean_attachment_fixture,  # <fixture> returns (fname, bytes) safe file
    phi_attachment_fixture,    # <fixture> returns (fname, bytes) PHI file
):
    # Case A: no attachment
    r_a = api_client.post("/messages", json={
        "subject": "DT-A", "body": "no attach", "provider_id": provider_id})
    assert r_a.status_code == 201

    # Case B: clean attachment
    cf_name, cf_bytes = clean_attachment_fixture
    r_b = api_client.post("/messages",
        data={"subject": "DT-B", "body": "clean", "provider_id": provider_id},
        files={"attachment": (cf_name, cf_bytes, "application/pdf")})
    assert r_b.status_code == 201

    # Case C: PHI attachment
    pf_name, pf_bytes = phi_attachment_fixture
    r_c = api_client.post("/messages",
        data={"subject": "DT-C", "body": "phi", "provider_id": provider_id},
        files={"attachment": (pf_name, pf_bytes, "application/pdf")})
    assert r_c.status_code in (400, 422)
    assert "PHI_EXFILTRATION_DETECTED" in r_c.json().get("error_code", "")

    msgs = api_client.get("/messages").json().get("messages", [])
    subjects = [m["subject"] for m in msgs]
    assert "DT-A" in subjects
    assert "DT-B" in subjects
    assert "DT-C" not in subjects
